mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates compress the equity discount rate and support risk assets by reducing the opportunity cost of holding stocks relative to bonds, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of non-overlapping periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions that boost consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates a self-reinforcing demand recovery where employment income drives consumption, producing stronger positive S&P 500 returns than either variable alone.
failure_condition: A portfolio long when both PAYEMS and RSAFS rise month-over-month does not outperform the average of the two single-signal portfolios by at least 2% annualized over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns during periods of monetary accommodation because low rates allow output gains to translate into margin expansion rather than inflation-driven tightening.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO fail to correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with rank IC > 0.04 in regimes where FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in month-over-month consumer price inflation above 0.3% raises expectations of future monetary tightening and increases the equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.3% month-over-month
regime_hint: zero lower bound with quantitative easing
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not negative in more than 55% of triggered instances when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales directly reflects nominal consumer demand, which drives aggregate revenue for S&P 500 constituents and generates positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month RSAFS growth and 20-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising housing starts and a falling unemployment rate signals a durable recovery in residential investment and labor income, jointly amplifying cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term of HOUST changes and UNRATE changes does not significantly improve R-squared by at least 0.02 for 60-day SPX returns relative to an additive linear model over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates contracting manufacturing output and capacity utilization, leading to negative earnings surprises and lower S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when INDPRO declines month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.
